sanskrit:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञाना: प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः।
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया।।
transliteration:
kāmais tais tair hṛta-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ
taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā
literal_meaning: Those whose knowledge is stolen by desires resort to other deities, following each respective rule, constrained by their own nature.
contextual_meaning: People who are emotionally regulated by unmet cravings outsource their inner authority to external ‘quick-fix’ powers (gods, gurus, gadgets, substances) and rigidly obey the rituals attached to them, remaining trapped in the personality patterns (gunas) that fuel the craving cycle.
psychological_state: Outsourced self-efficacy: the ego, fragmented by desire, seeks symbolic parent-figures (demigods) to regulate affect, creating compulsive rule-following that reinforces the original insecurity.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling at 2 a.m., buying the fifth online course that promises ‘six-figure passive income in 30 days’, then obsessively following its checklist while still feeling hollow.
anchor_text: Own your inner compass; craving-driven rituals keep you spinning in the same groove.
